A property manager who wants to keep the same paid account may instead request a workspace reset. This permanently removes the current portfolio and operational data after a 24-hour cancellation period while preserving the login, plan, subscription, billing relationship, and purchased unit allowance.

A reset removes properties, units, resident and vendor records, staff, work orders, inspections, messages, uploads, reports, schedules, portal access, and connected integrations. It does not cancel billing or change your plan.

Simeonware and its licensors retain ownership of the service, software, branding, and documentation. You retain ownership of content you lawfully submit and grant Simeonware a limited license to host, process, reproduce, and transmit that content only as needed to operate, secure, support, and improve the service.

Simeonware collects account details, contact information, property and unit records, tenant and vendor details, requests, messages, photos, files, schedules, inspections, billing records, integration data, device identifiers, and security and audit events needed to operate the service.
Simeonware creates the work order before it is due, then reminds the manager daily if it remains open after the due date.

Simeonware does not sell personal information. Information is shared with authorized participants in a maintenance workflow and with providers that supply hosting, databases, private storage, email, authentication, monitoring, payment, calendar, mailbox, accounting, and similar operating services.
Simeonware gives property managers one place to receive tenant requests, invite vendors, approve bids, track photos and costs, and know the next step on every job.
Simeonware is built for the maintenance work property managers repeat every day: tenant intake, vendor bids, approvals, photos, billing records, closeout notes, and CSV movement for custom systems.
Simeonware maintenance request showing vendor coordination and the next manager action

Simeonware provides coordination, communication, scheduling, recordkeeping, inspection, billing-support, and integration tools. It does not provide repair, emergency, property-management, legal, tax, or accounting services. Property managers remain responsible for emergency response, legal compliance, vendor selection, approvals, payments, tenant communications, and reviewing system records.
Simeonware remains the source of truth and automatically publishes its schedule to one Outlook calendar.
Simeonware request detail showing vendor coordination signals

Simeonware sends trial-ending reminders approximately seven and two days before expiration. If no paid subscription is purchased, subscription-controlled manager access is paused and no charge is made. Information remains subject to the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.

The 30 days begin when your Simeonware account is created. The onboarding conversation is scheduled within the first few business days when calendars allow, and does not extend the trial.

The 30-Day Assisted Trial is available by invitation to businesses managing property in the 50 United States or District of Columbia. One trial is normally available per business or related business group.

The customer selects an intended plan and billing frequency during signup, but that selection is not a payment authorization. If the customer purchases the selected service before the trial expires, the base subscription price and additional-unit rate shown for that purchase will remain unchanged for 12 months from the first paid subscription date. Taxes, customer-requested plan changes, added capacity, and separately approved services are excluded.

The service may be unavailable during maintenance, outages, provider failures, or emergencies.
